sources.label: Interroll press release - order intake accelerates (FY2025: +5.0% to CHF 545.3m, +9.2% lc)
Investing.com - Berenberg Buy rating on warehouse automation inflection (pre-existing, not new)
TipRanks/The Fly - Berenberg PT cut to CHF 2,500 (Feb 2026, kept Buy)

verdicts.rankRationale: No material news since June 6 and today's +0.82% is drift; yesterday's hold already reconciled the YTD de-rating, so no new information justifies a re-rank. The -34.7% YTD washout against a stabilizing order-intake picture (FY2025 orders +9.2% lc, accelerating in H2) marginally improves asymmetry for this latent-node lens, worth a small positive drift but not a move.
verdicts.bull: Order intake inflected (+9.2% lc, accelerating into H2 2025) while the stock has been cut by a third YTD; if e-commerce and sorter project demand keeps recovering, an under-owned CHF 1.7B pick-and-shovel re-rates sharply. Berenberg still carries a Buy with a CHF 2,500 target versus ~CHF 1,472 today.
verdicts.neutral: Known scheduled items only: CHF 32 dividend goes ex June 16 and long-serving CFO Heinz Hossli departs end of June. The automation recovery is real but slow, and the stock can chop sideways until order momentum shows up in revenue and margins.
verdicts.bear: Project business remains hostage to macro capex timing; a stalled e-commerce recovery or tariff-driven capex deferrals would invalidate the inflection narrative, and the CFO transition adds execution uncertainty at a fragile moment.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: H1 2026 results / order-intake print confirming the recovery is accelerating into revenue.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Warehouse-automation capex recovery stalls, leaving the order-intake inflection a false start while the CFO seat turns over.
